content.text:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to describe a target word for another player to guess.

Rules:
(a) You have to reply in the form: CLUE: <some text>. Guesses from the other player will start with GUESS.
(b) You cannot use the target word itself, parts or morphological variants of it in your description.
(c) In addition, the same rules apply for related words which are provided below.

End conditions:
(i) If you use the target word or a related word in your description, then you lose.
(ii) If the other player can guess the target word in 3 tries, you both win.

Let us start.

This is the target word that you need to describe and that the other player needs to guess:

reach

Related words are:

- stretch
- extend
- distance

Important: You are under time pressure, give short descriptions that are to the point!

content.thinking:
I need to describe the word "reach" without using:
- reach
- stretch
- extend
- distance

I should think of what "reach" means:
- To arrive at or get to something
- To touch something by putting your hand/arm out
- To make contact with something
- To achieve or attain something

Short, concise clue needed. Let me think:
- "Arrive at a destination"
- "Touch something by putting your arm out"
- "Grasp something far away"
- "Get to a place or object"

I'll go with something about grasping or touching something that's somewhat far.

content.text: CLUE: To arrive at or touch something by putting your arm out toward it

clem_player.response: CLUE: To arrive at or touch something by putting your arm out toward it

content.text:
You are playing a collaborative word guessing game in which you have to guess a target word that another player describes to you.

You can make one guess at each trial. You win when you guess the target word. You lose when you cannot guess it in 3 tries.

After each trial you will get a new hint from the other player which starts with CLUE.

Make your guesses by just saying the word using the following form: GUESS: <a word>

Let us start.


CLUE: To arrive at or touch something by putting your arm out toward it

content.thinking:
The clue describes an action where you put your arm out toward something to arrive at or touch it. This sounds like the word "reach" - when you reach for something, you extend your arm to touch or grab it.

Let me think if there are other possibilities:
- "touch" - but this doesn't emphasize the arm extension
- "grab" - this is more about grasping than extending
- "reach" - this fits perfectly with extending your arm to arrive at or touch something

I'm confident the answer is "reach".
